paragraphs.en: Good morning, everyone! This is Maya. She is a new student in our class. Maya, please tell us about yourself.
paragraphs.fr: Bonjour a tous ! Voici Maya. C'est une nouvelle eleve dans notre classe. Maya, s'il te plait, parle-nous de toi.
paragraphs.speaker: Teacher

paragraphs.en: Hello! My name is Maya. I moved here last month with my family. Before, I lived in Canada. I spoke English and French there.
paragraphs.fr: Bonjour ! Je m'appelle Maya. J'ai demenage ici le mois dernier avec ma famille. Avant, j'habitais au Canada. Je parlais anglais et francais la-bas.
paragraphs.speaker: Maya

paragraphs.en: That's cool! Where did you live in Canada? Did you like it there?
paragraphs.fr: C'est cool ! Ou est-ce que tu habitais au Canada ? Tu aimais la-bas ?
paragraphs.speaker: Liam

paragraphs.en: I lived in Montreal. Yes, I liked it a lot! But my dad got a new job here, so we moved. It was very different at first.
paragraphs.fr: J'habitais a Montreal. Oui, j'aimais beaucoup ! Mais mon papa a eu un nouveau travail ici, alors nous avons demenage. C'etait tres different au debut.
paragraphs.speaker: Maya

paragraphs.en: Were you worried about the new school? I was worried when I changed schools too.
paragraphs.fr: Tu etais inquiete pour la nouvelle ecole ? Moi aussi j'etais inquiet quand j'ai change d'ecole.
paragraphs.speaker: Liam

paragraphs.en: Yes, I was a little worried. But everyone here is very friendly and welcoming. I feel much better now. Thank you!
paragraphs.fr: Oui, j'etais un peu inquiete. Mais tout le monde ici est tres gentil et accueillant. Je me sens beaucoup mieux maintenant. Merci !
paragraphs.speaker: Maya

paragraphs.en: Welcome to our class, Maya! Don't worry, you are going to love it here. We are happy to have you!
paragraphs.fr: Bienvenue dans notre classe, Maya ! Ne t'inquiete pas, tu vas adorer ici. Nous sommes contents de t'avoir !
paragraphs.speaker: Teacher

vocabulary.definition_en: passe de 'move' — aller vivre dans un autre endroit
vocabulary.example: We moved to a new city last year.

vocabulary.definition_en: a un moment plus tot, dans le passe
un territoire avec son propre gouvernement, comme la France ou le Canada
vocabulary.example: Canada is a very big country.

vocabulary.definition_en: passe de 'live' — avoir sa maison dans un endroit
vocabulary.example: I lived in Montreal for five years.

vocabulary.definition_en: passe de 'speak' — utiliser une langue ou parler
vocabulary.example: She spoke French and English in Canada.

vocabulary.definition_en: pas pareil qu'une autre chose ou personne
vocabulary.example: The new school was very different from my old one.

vocabulary.definition_en: qui se sent anxieux ou preoccupe par quelque chose
vocabulary.example: I was worried about the exam.

vocabulary.definition_en: gentil et agreable avec les autres
vocabulary.example: The people here are very friendly.

vocabulary.definition_en: recu avec plaisir ; une salutation pour quelqu'un qui arrive

grammar_points.title: Past simple questions with 'did'
grammar_points.explanation: Pour poser des questions au passe, on utilise **Did + sujet + forme de base** du verbe (PAS la forme du passe). 'Did' est le meme pour tous les sujets. Pour les questions en 'Wh-', on met le mot interrogatif en premier : **Where did you live?**
examples: Did you like it? = Est-ce que tu aimais ? (NOT: Did you liked it?)
Where did you live? = Ou est-ce que tu habitais ?
Did she speak French? = Est-ce qu'elle parlait francais ?
What did you do there? = Qu'est-ce que tu faisais la-bas ?

grammar_points.title: Past simple of 'be' in questions
grammar_points.explanation: Pour les questions avec 'was/were', on **inverse simplement le sujet et le verbe** : Was she worried? Were they happy? On n'utilise PAS 'did' avec 'was/were'.
examples: Were you worried? = Tu etais inquiet(e) ? (NOT: Did you be worried?)
Was she happy? = Elle etait contente ?
Where were you yesterday? = Ou etais-tu hier ?

expressions.example: Where are you from? — I'm from Canada.
expressions.note: La facon la plus courante de demander a quelqu'un d'ou il vient (pays ou ville).

expressions.example: I used to live in Montreal, but now I live here.
expressions.note: 'Used to' decrit quelque chose qui etait vrai dans le passe mais qui ne l'est plus maintenant. Cette forme n'existe qu'au passe.

expressions.meaning_fr: Ne t'inquiete pas / Ne vous inquietez pas
expressions.example: Don't worry, everything will be fine!
expressions.note: S'utilise pour rassurer quelqu'un qui est anxieux ou nerveux.

written.question: Where did Maya live before?
written.explanation: Maya says 'Before, I lived in Canada.'

written.question: Which question is correct?
written.explanation: Past simple questions use: Did + subject + base form. So: 'Where did you live?' (NOT 'lived').

written.question: How do you ask 'Tu etais inquiet(e) ?' in English?
written.explanation: With 'be', we don't use 'did'. We reverse subject and verb: 'Were you worried?'

written.sentence: ________ you like your old school? — Yes, I liked it a lot.
written.hint: Use the helping verb for past simple questions.

written.sentence: Where ________ she live before? — She lived in Montreal.
written.hint: Use the helping verb for past simple questions with 'Wh-' words.

written.sentence: ________ you worried about the new school? — Yes, I was a little worried.
written.hint: With 'be' in the past, just reverse the subject and verb. 'You' uses which form of 'be'?

written.french: Ou est-ce que tu habitais avant ? J'habitais au Canada.
written.suggested_answer: Where did you live before? I lived in Canada.

written.french: Est-ce que tu aimais ton ancienne ecole ?
written.suggested_answer: Did you like your old school?

oral.instruction: Read this dialogue aloud. Try to use natural intonation for questions.
oral.text: Where did you live before? — I lived in Montreal. Did you like it there? — Yes, I liked it a lot! Were you worried about the new school? — Yes, a little, but everyone is very friendly.
oral.focus: Questions go UP in intonation at the end: 'Did you LIKE it?' Questions with Wh- words go DOWN: 'WHERE did you live?'

oral.question: Imagine you are a new student. Where did you live before? Did you like your old school?
oral.suggested_answer: Before, I lived in Paris. I liked my old school a lot because my friends were there. I was a little worried about the new school, but the teacher was very friendly.

oral.question: Ask me three questions about my past using 'did' or 'was/were'.
oral.suggested_answer: Where did you live before? Did you like your old school? Were you happy there?
